steps.title: Find the total years worked by all 20 employees
steps.content:
Use the mean formula: sum of all values equals mean times the count.
$$\text{Sum}_{20} = 6.7 \times 20 = 134 \text{ years}$$

steps.title: Find the total years worked by the remaining 19 employees
steps.content:
After one employee leaves, the remaining 19 have a mean of 6.25 years:
$$\text{Sum}_{19} = 6.25 \times 19 = 118.75 \text{ years}$$

steps.title: Find the departing employee's years of service
steps.content:
Subtract the remaining sum from the original sum:
$$\text{Departing employee's years} = 134 - 118.75 = 15.25$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
